plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-547 RP17.P09.M03.S11-RP17.P09.M05.S08 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-547 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP17.P09 workflow permission slice: the M03 Primary Implementation Slice tail (the twelve remaining rows), the full M04 Secondary Workflow Slice cycle (twenty rows), and the M05 Permission And Audit Binding head (the first eight rows) as three descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H17), Claude review packet (C17), and CP00-548 handoff, without opening AI governance, model policy, retrieval scope, or citation runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-547 is a descriptor-only RP17.P09 workflow permission slice pack (risk B, 40 units, RP17.P09.M03.S11-RP17.P09.M05.S08). The registry pack binding (registry.js:562), contract current_pack + p09_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor projection, README section, service descriptor/case-set, validators, and tests are mutually consistent and match the live plan's deliverable distribution (impl 25, claude_review 6, hermes_evidence 1, security_audit 4, test 2, ui 2), micro-title counts (12/20/8), micro-phase counts (M03 12, M04 20, M05 8), and all 40 section-row titles exactly. All 7 required public exports exist (2 in service.js, 5 in validators.js) and are re-exported through index.js export *. The validator (validators.js:6013-6158) enforces pack identity, distribution drift, descriptor-only row guards (descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false), the no-write attestation, the authority boundary, contract-projection drift, and handoff target/next-subphase. Tests load the plan pack from the real closeout-pack-plan.json and assert coverage, descriptor-only rows, authority boundaries, and evidence/handoff. No-write/no-real-data attestations are exhaustive (writes_product_state=false, no_real_data=true, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, loads_real_fixture_data=false). Authority boundary is preserved: Claude is read-only and not final approver, human approval is required for runtime opening, and no enterprise-trust claim is made from local validation. CP178+ review hardening is encoded via forbidden_review_evidence (auth failures, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing references, final-approval/source-mutation/enterprise-trust claims), and the readiness auth-preflight passed. Hermes patterns are confined to the deterministic evidence packet/receipt, not product runtime. command-evidence.json records every validation gate passing (exit 0) with production_ready honestly false and status implemented_pending_review. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; two P3 informational notes only.

findings.title: Post-review deliverables referenced in manifest are not yet present on disk (expected at review stage)
findings.details: Each unit in the manifest references deliverables docs/closeout-packs/cp00-547/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json. Only manifest.json and command-evidence.json currently exist in that directory. This is consistent with the hardened_review_sequence (read_only_claude_run -> receipt_normalization -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection -> final_closeout_validation -> commit): these artifacts are produced from this review and later steps, not before. Not a defect; noted so closeout verifies their presence before the final commit gate.
findings.recommendation: Ensure claude-review-result.json (derived from this PASS verdict), adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json are generated and validated by the post-review gated commands before committing the pack.

findings.title: Unrelated dirty/untracked files present in working tree but correctly excluded from pack scope
findings.details: The working tree contains modified scripts/progress-control-room-data.mjs and tools/progress-control-room/index.html plus several untracked workbook/* and docs/* files. changed-file-scope.json correctly lists these under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved and they are not in active_pack_implementation_refs, so per instructions they are not part of CP00-547 (progress-control-room is the separate UI/non-CP workstream). No pack content depends on them.
findings.recommendation: When staging CP00-547, restrict the commit to the active_pack_implementation_refs (ai-governance package, contract, validator script) plus the pack docs, and avoid sweeping the unrelated dirty/untracked files into the pack commit.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-547 ai governance core p09 workflow permission slice descriptor pack
